source_jobs.title: Senior Field and Experiential Marketing Manager
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US:

Brisk Teaching is the leading AI platform for K-12 educators, empowering teachers to deliver personalized, curriculum-aligned instruction at scale. Our Classroom Intelligence tools connect teachers, students, and curriculum—making meaningful learning happen in classrooms every day. We believe the best technology is cutting-edge, respects teachers’ time, integrates into real classrooms, and helps educators focus on what matters most.

Since launching, Brisk has experienced rapid growth. Over 2 million teachers use our platform, and we partner with districts and schools nationwide to transform how educators teach and how students learn.

We're backed by leading venture and edtech investors including Bessemer Venture Partners (Shopify, LinkedIn, Anthropic, Perplexity) and Owl Ventures (Newsela, Quizlet, MasterClass), with a team spanning top AI companies and real classroom experience.

We're a fast-moving, collaborative team driven by a shared commitment to educator impact and product excellence. Our values—urgency, ownership, and focus—shape how we build and who we are.




THE OPPORTUNITY:

We're looking for a Senior Field & Experiential Marketing Manager who gets genuinely excited about the full arc — from a wild idea on a whiteboard to the moment a teacher posts a picture of your activation on Instagram and tags all their friends.

This is not a "manage the booth vendor and ship the banners" role. This is a creative and strategic builder role — one that's as much about inventing new ways to show up for teachers as it is about executing the playbook we've already started.

Conference and trade show presence is part of it. But the bigger opportunity is everything beyond that: surprise-and-delight activations in teacher communities and own Brisk events, pop-ups, grassroots moments, and formats the edtech world hasn't seen yet.

You'll be as comfortable hauling equipment and problem-solving on-site as you are pitching a bold new concept to leadership or pulling post-event attribution data. You think in experiences, not checkboxes. Conferences are just the beginning.




WHAT WE'VE BUILT:

We're not starting from zero. Here's a taste of what Brisk has already pulled off — and why we need someone to take it to the next level.

The Brisk-It Barbeque — Our flagship conference booth isn't a booth. It's a backyard. Fake grass, hay bale seating, picnic tables, string lights, marquee letter signage, a brick backdrop, and a photo wall built for sharing. At our last major conference, we ran a full programming schedule out of the space — named sessions, educator creator takeovers, live feature drops, and a Slam Poetry Night. Teachers didn't just stop by. They came back.

Swag teachers actually keep — We think hard about what ends up in a teacher's bag versus a hotel trash can. The result is merch people seek out at our booth, show off on social, and carry into their classrooms. We're looking for someone who raises the bar on this every single time.

The bar is already high. Your job is to raise it.



[https://app.ashbyhq.com/api/images/user-content/2c6b2170-4135-474d-b683-0d3985d2ab48/924b683e-6011-4eea-bd56-3ae3506f7294/Screenshot%202026-04-09%20at%201.12.28%E2%80%AFPM.png]
[https://app.ashbyhq.com/api/images/user-content/2c6b2170-4135-474d-b683-0d3985d2ab48/de0474f4-1f3d-4f6d-8d6b-bfedfaf13d2b/Screenshot%202026-04-09%20at%201.12.38%E2%80%AFPM.png]




YOU WILL:

 - End-to-End Execution
   
   - Own logistics from concept to load-out: venue/vendor sourcing, budget management, permitting, staffing, swag, setup, and teardown.
   
   - Be the person on the ground making it work — and make it look effortless.
   
   - Build repeatable playbooks so great activations can scale.
   
   Experiential Strategy & Creative Ideation
   
   - Take ownership of Brisk's field and events strategy and make it better — conference presence, owned events, community activations, and formats we haven't tried yet.
   
   - Dream up and pitch bold, mem

source_jobs.title: Lead Infrastructure Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US:

Brisk Teaching is the leading AI platform for K-12 educators, empowering teachers to deliver personalized, curriculum-aligned instruction at scale. Our Classroom Intelligence tools connect teachers, students, and curriculum—making meaningful learning happen in classrooms every day. We believe the best technology is cutting-edge, respects teachers’ time, integrates into real classrooms, and helps educators focus on what matters most.

Since launching, Brisk has experienced rapid growth. Over 2 million teachers use our platform, and we partner with districts and schools nationwide to transform how educators teach and how students learn.

We're backed by leading venture and edtech investors including Bessemer Venture Partners (Shopify, LinkedIn, Anthropic, Perplexity) and Owl Ventures (Newsela, Quizlet, MasterClass), with a team spanning top AI companies and real classroom experience.

We're a fast-moving, collaborative team driven by a shared commitment to educator impact and product excellence. Our values—urgency, ownership, and focus—shape how we build and who we are.




THE OPPORTUNITY:

We're looking for a leader to own and grow Brisk's Platform team — the group responsible for the core infrastructure, shared services, and developer experience that everything else at Brisk is built on.

We’re looking for someone who is excited to jump into leading the day-to-day of the current Platform team members my while staying close to the technical work. You'll work closely with our team on driving forward our roadmap, collaborating and coordinating with the rest of our engineering work, and making sure that the Platform team is keeping Brisk reliable as we scale. You'll also develop the engineers who keep it all running.

This is a player-coach role on a small, high-impact team. You'll work closely with product, design, and leadership, and you'll have real ownership from day one.




YOU WILL:

 - Own the roadmap and partner with our team on technical direction for Brisk's platform — core infrastructure, CI/CD, developer tooling, and shared services.

 - Mentor, and grow a small team of platform/infrastructure engineers (or grow into managing one, if you join as an IC).

 - Stay hands-on: design, build, and maintain the AWS-based infrastructure that powers our products.

 - Drive reliability and scalability so performance stays seamless as our user base expands.

 - Lead observability, monitoring, incident response, and on-call practices.

 - Partner with product engineers to keep development workflows smooth and production stable.

 - Make the architectural calls that balance rapid product development with long-term reliability and security.




YOU HAVE:

 - 7+ years in software engineering with a strong focus on infrastructure.

 - People management experience, or a track record as a tech lead/mentor with a clear desire to people manage.

 - Experience designing scalable, cloud-native architectures on AWS (EC2, S3, Lambda, RDS, etc.).

 - Strong knowledge of CI/CD pipelines, deployment automation, and monitoring tools.

 - A problem-solving mentality — comfortable moving between infra, backend, and occasional product work.

 - Strong written and oral communication, a proactive mindset, and the ability to operate independently in ambiguity.




BONUS POINTS:

 - Experience building or leading a Platform / Infrastructure / DevEx team.

 - Background in security best practices for infrastructure and data.

 - Previous experience in a startup environment or small, fast-moving team.

 - Experience working with AI-powered systems or integrating AI into infrastructure.

 - Experience with analytics infrastructure and data pipelines.

 - Experience managing cloud costs.

Equal Employment Opportunity & Diversity

At Brisk, we believe that diverse perspectives make better teams and stronger products. We are an equal opportunity employer and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. All qualified applic

source_jobs.title: Senior Growth Marketing Manager (B2C)
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US:

Brisk Teaching is the leading AI platform for K-12 educators, empowering teachers to deliver personalized, curriculum-aligned instruction at scale. Our Classroom Intelligence tools connect teachers, students, and curriculum—making meaningful learning happen in classrooms every day. We believe the best technology is cutting-edge, respects teachers’ time, integrates into real classrooms, and helps educators focus on what matters most.

Since launching, Brisk has experienced rapid growth. Over 2 million teachers use our platform, and we partner with districts and schools nationwide to transform how educators teach and how students learn.

We're backed by leading venture and edtech investors including Bessemer Venture Partners (Shopify, LinkedIn, Anthropic, Perplexity) and Owl Ventures (Newsela, Quizlet, MasterClass), with a team spanning top AI companies and real classroom experience.

We're a fast-moving, collaborative team driven by a shared commitment to educator impact and product excellence. Our values—urgency, ownership, and focus—shape how we build and who we are.




THE OPPORTUNITY:

This is one of the most strategic individual contributor roles at Brisk, reporting directly to the VP of Marketing.

You'll own educator growth from first impression through activation—defining the strategy, building the roadmap, executing high-impact initiatives, and measuring what drives results. From acquisition and conversion to experimentation, attribution, and activation, you'll shape how Brisk reaches millions more educators.

This is a true builder role. There isn't an established growth function or mature playbook waiting for you to inherit. You'll define what great looks like, build the systems that make growth repeatable, and partner closely with Product, Design, Engineering, Brand, and Product Marketing to bring that vision to life.

We're looking for someone with exceptional judgment, a bias toward action, and the ability to move seamlessly between strategy and execution. If you're energized by solving hard growth problems, building from the ground up, and creating measurable business impact, you'll thrive here.




YOU WILL:


GROWTH STRATEGY & ROADMAP

Define Brisk's growth strategy and roadmap. Identify the highest-leverage opportunities to accelerate educator acquisition, prioritize investments, and turn ambitious growth goals into measurable outcomes.




WEBSITE & CONVERSION

Treat briskteaching.com http://briskteaching.com like a product — not a marketing site.

Own the website roadmap, messaging, information architecture, landing pages, and conversion strategy. Launch improvements through Webflow, optimize the user journey, and continuously improve conversion through experimentation.




SEARCH & DISCOVERABILITY (SEO + AEO)

Lead Brisk's organic growth strategy across traditional search and AI-powered discovery.

Develop strategies across SEO, Answer Engine Optimization (AEO), and emerging search experiences to ensure educators discover Brisk wherever they're looking.




PERFORMANCE MARKETING

Own Brisk's paid acquisition strategy across digital channels.

Allocate budget, launch campaigns, test new opportunities, and optimize performance to maximize efficient growth. Know when to scale investment — and when to shift resources elsewhere.




GROWTH ANALYTICS & MEASUREMENT

Build the measurement system that powers growth.

Develop dashboards and reporting that give leadership clear visibility into:

 - Growth performance

 - CAC and acquisition efficiency

 - Channel attribution

 - Funnel conversion

 - Marketing ROI

 - Activation

 - Experiment performance

Translate complex growth data into clear recommendations that help leadership make confident investment decisions.




EXPERIMENTATION & OPTIMIZATION

Build a culture of rapid experimentation across the acquisition funnel.

Develop hypotheses, launch tests, measure outcomes, and scale what works. Balance speed with rigor while continuously improving perf

source_jobs.title: Lead Data Scientist
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US:

Brisk Teaching is the leading AI platform for K-12 educators, empowering teachers to deliver personalized, curriculum-aligned instruction at scale. Our Classroom Intelligence tools connect teachers, students, and curriculum—making meaningful learning happen in classrooms every day. We believe the best technology is cutting-edge, respects teachers’ time, integrates into real classrooms, and helps educators focus on what matters most.

Since launching, Brisk has experienced rapid growth. Over 2 million teachers use our platform, and we partner with districts and schools nationwide to transform how educators teach and how students learn.

We're backed by leading venture and edtech investors including Bessemer Venture Partners (Shopify, LinkedIn, Anthropic, Perplexity) and Owl Ventures (Newsela, Quizlet, MasterClass), with a team spanning top AI companies and real classroom experience.

We're a fast-moving, collaborative team driven by a shared commitment to educator impact and product excellence. Our values—urgency, ownership, and focus—shape how we build and who we are.




THE OPPORTUNITY:

We've already started building something we think fundamentally rethinks how data science gets done at a company. We have an internal data agent that can query our warehouse, interpret results, and surface insights to stakeholders directly. It works. People use it. And it's changed how our team operates day to day.

Now we need someone to take it further. As Brisk's product and organization grow, the systems underneath need to grow with them: a more robust semantic layer, better tooling for experimentation, more sophisticated automation across the data lifecycle, and the strategic thinking to decide where AI amplifies the team's work and where it replaces manual processes entirely.

This role is equal parts strategy and execution. You'll own the vision for how data drives decisions across Brisk, scale the AI-native workflows we've started, and build the systems that turn a small data team into one that operates like a much larger org. You'll report directly to leadership and have a seat at the table on product strategy, go-to-market, and company priorities.




YOU WILL:

 - Set the data strategy: Define what we measure, how we measure it, and what "good" looks like. Establish the metrics architecture that connects product usage, retention, monetization, and growth into a coherent picture, and make sure every team at Brisk is oriented around it.

 - Turn the data team into a product team: Build internal data products that stakeholders across the company actually use daily. Replace ad-hoc requests with self-serve AI interfaces, automated reports, and tools that make data accessible to non-technical teammates.

 - Make the warehouse AI-readable: Build the semantic layer, documentation, and context infrastructure that lets both humans and AI systems query Brisk's data accurately. A well-documented warehouse is the foundation for every AI-powered workflow that follows.

 - Build AI into the data lifecycle: Identify where AI agents, automated pipelines, and LLM-powered tooling can replace manual work: dbt model generation, data quality monitoring, experiment analysis, insight delivery. Ship these systems into production.

 - Own product analytics and experimentation: Partner with Product, Engineering, and Design to design experiments, interpret results, and deliver the insights that shape what we build. Bring rigor to how we evaluate features and make ship/no-ship decisions.

 - Drive growth and business intelligence: Maintain and evolve dashboards and reporting for Sales, Marketing, Customer Success, and leadership. Ensure the metrics that matter are visible, trusted, and actionable.

 - Scale through systems, not headcount: Build infrastructure and AI-powered tooling that multiplies the team's output so a small data org can support a fast-growing company without scaling linearly.




YOU HAVE:

 - 8+ years of experience in data scien

source_jobs.title: Staff Product Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US:

Brisk Teaching is the leading AI platform for K-12 educators, empowering teachers to deliver personalized, curriculum-aligned instruction at scale. Our Classroom Intelligence tools connect teachers, students, and curriculum—making meaningful learning happen in classrooms every day. We believe the best technology is cutting-edge, respects teachers’ time, integrates into real classrooms, and helps educators focus on what matters most.

Since launching, Brisk has experienced rapid growth. Over 2 million teachers use our platform, and we partner with districts and schools nationwide to transform how educators teach and how students learn.

We're backed by leading venture and edtech investors including Bessemer Venture Partners (Shopify, LinkedIn, Anthropic, Perplexity) and Owl Ventures (Newsela, Quizlet, MasterClass), with a team spanning top AI companies and real classroom experience.

We're a fast-moving, collaborative team driven by a shared commitment to educator impact and product excellence. Our values—urgency, ownership, and focus—shape how we build and who we are.




THE OPPORTUNITY:

We're looking for a Staff Product Engineer to set the bar for the craft, architecture, and quality of everything teachers and students see and touch in Brisk. You'll own the frontend at a deep technical level — the React patterns we lean on, the browser-extension surface that brings Brisk into the tools educators already use, and the performance and accessibility standards we hold the product to.

This is a senior individual contributor role with broad influence. You'll shape direction across our frontend codebase, raise the bar through code review and mentorship, and ship features that millions of teachers rely on every day. The Brisk Extension lives inside Google Docs, Slides, YouTube, Microsoft Word and PowerPoint online, and a growing list of other surfaces, which means the frontend is, in many ways, the product itself.




YOU WILL:

 - Set technical direction for our frontend architecture, patterns, and tooling, and bring the team along with you.

 - Lead complex UI work in React and TypeScript across the Brisk Extension, Brisk Next, and Brisk Boost.

 - Push the Brisk Extension forward across Google Workspace, Microsoft 365, and beyond — DOM integration, content scripts, message passing, storage APIs, manifest evolution, and the browser APIs that make our in-the-tool experience possible.

 - Reverse-engineer external surfaces when the documented APIs don't go far enough — read the network calls, study the DOM, and find the seams that let us build integrations our competitors can't.

 - Stand up the telemetry, instrumentation, and active monitoring that keep us honest — frontend logging, error tracking, real user monitoring, and Datadog dashboards that tell us what's happening before users do.

 - Own frontend performance: bundle size, rendering paths, runtime profiling, and Core Web Vitals. Set the targets, build the dashboards, and ship the changes that move the numbers.

 - Champion accessibility as a baseline rather than an afterthought, so every teacher and every student can use what we build — semantic HTML, ARIA, keyboard navigation, and screen reader support are expected, not bolted on.

 - Build and evolve the Brisk design system so the rest of the team ships faster and the product feels coherent across surfaces.

 - Define our testing strategy across unit, integration, and end-to-end layers, and keep the suite fast and trustworthy as the codebase grows.

 - Mentor engineers, raise the bar on code review, and partner closely with design, product, and the founding team to shape what we build.

 - Contribute to backend services in Python and Flask when a feature needs it, and stay close to the API contracts that connect frontend to back.

 - Devise database persistence architecture, including RDBMS schema or KVS key structure, to store data backing high traffic interfaces.




YOU HAVE:

 - 8+ years of professional so

source_jobs.title: Mid-Market Account Executive
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US:

Brisk Teaching is the leading AI platform for K-12 educators, empowering teachers to deliver personalized, curriculum-aligned instruction at scale. Our Classroom Intelligence tools connect teachers, students, and curriculum—making meaningful learning happen in classrooms every day. We believe the best technology is cutting-edge, respects teachers’ time, integrates into real classrooms, and helps educators focus on what matters most.

Since launching, Brisk has experienced rapid growth. Over 2 million teachers use our platform, and we partner with districts and schools nationwide to transform how educators teach and how students learn.

We're backed by leading venture and edtech investors including Bessemer Venture Partners (Shopify, LinkedIn, Anthropic, Perplexity) and Owl Ventures (Newsela, Quizlet, MasterClass), with a team spanning top AI companies and real classroom experience.

We're a fast-moving, collaborative team driven by a shared commitment to educator impact and product excellence. Our values—urgency, ownership, and focus—shape how we build and who we are.




THE OPPORTUNITY:

We’re looking for a dynamic and results-driven Mid-Market Account Executive to join our team. In this role, you’ll be responsible for identifying, engaging, and closing new school and district partnerships. You’ll play a critical part in expanding Brisk’s footprint, working closely with potential clients to understand their needs and demonstrate how Brisk’s solutions can enhance their teaching and learning outcomes.




YOU WILL:

 - Lead Generation & Outreach: Identify and prospect new school and district accounts through targeted outreach, leveraging existing networks, and participating in educational events.

 - Sales Process Management: Guide prospective clients through the sales journey, from initial contact to closing, ensuring clear communication and a tailored approach.

 - Relationship Building: Develop strong relationships with education leaders, understanding their challenges and goals, and positioning Brisk’s offerings as valuable solutions.

 - Travel: Travel approximately 25-30% of the time to meet with prospective schools and districts, attend educational conferences in the territory, and represent Brisk at industry events.

 - Collaboration: Work closely with the Customer Success, Product, and Marketing teams to ensure a smooth onboarding process and a seamless transition from prospect to customer.

 - Market Insights: Gather insights from potential customers to share with our product and leadership teams, helping shape future product developments and strategies.




YOU HAVE:

 - BA or BS in any field.

 - Sales Experience: Proven quota achievement record in B2B sales, with at least 2+ years of experience selling into EdTech specifically.

 - Tools Experience: Prior familiarity, and ideally work experience, with Salesforce, Gong, and Outreach.

 - Location: a WFH location in CA or a CT, MT, or PT time zone.

 - Communication Skills: Excellent written and verbal communication skills with the ability to connect with diverse stakeholders.

 - Customer-Focused: Passionate about understanding and solving customer challenges, with a genuine interest in K-12 education.

 - Self-Motivated: Ability to work independently and thrive in a fast-paced, evolving environment.

 - Team-Oriented: Comfortable collaborating with cross-functional teams to ensure the best outcomes for our clients and company.




BONUS:

 - Classroom teaching experience.

 - MEDDPICC certification.

Equal Employment Opportunity & Diversity

At Brisk, we believe that diverse perspectives make better teams and stronger products. We are an equal opportunity employer and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, age, national origin, veteran status, disability, or any

source_jobs.title: Senior Business Operations Manager
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US:

Brisk Teaching is the leading AI platform for K-12 educators, empowering teachers to deliver personalized, curriculum-aligned instruction at scale. Our Classroom Intelligence tools connect teachers, students, and curriculum—making meaningful learning happen in classrooms every day. We believe the best technology is cutting-edge, respects teachers’ time, integrates into real classrooms, and helps educators focus on what matters most.

Since launching, Brisk has experienced rapid growth. Over 2 million teachers use our platform, and we partner with districts and schools nationwide to transform how educators teach and how students learn.

We're backed by leading venture and edtech investors including Bessemer Venture Partners (Shopify, LinkedIn, Anthropic, Perplexity) and Owl Ventures (Newsela, Quizlet, MasterClass), with a team spanning top AI companies and real classroom experience.

We're a fast-moving, collaborative team driven by a shared commitment to educator impact and product excellence. Our values—urgency, ownership, and focus—shape how we build and who we are.




THE OPPORTUNITY:

We run a lean operations team that covers finance, people, legal, IT, and business operations for the entire company. We don't have layers of management or detailed playbooks for every scenario — we figure things out, build the right system, and move on to the next problem. You'll thrive here if you like owning your work end-to-end and bringing your own structure to open-ended problems.

As Senior Business Operations Manager, you'll own finance operations and financial planning across the company. You'll drive budget accountability with department leads, build the models that inform how we spend and grow, and manage the vendors and procurement processes that keep the business running efficiently. You'll report to our Head of Operations and partner closely with our fractional CFO, but day-to-day you'll operate with a high degree of autonomy — identifying what needs to happen, building the approach, and driving it to completion.

This is a small team that punches well above its weight, and we use AI aggressively to stay that way. You should be the kind of person who reaches for Claude or builds a workflow before defaulting to a spreadsheet and manual effort.




YOU WILL:

Finance operations

 - Own accounts receivable end-to-end, including billing, collections, and reconciliation.

 - Manage accounts payable, including invoice processing and vendor payments.

 - Manage the relationship with our external bookkeeper and tax advisors, serving as the primary internal point of contact for routine finance, tax, and compliance work — including monthly close support, reconciliation, and renewal coordination.

 - Administer our expense platform (Ramp), including program enforcement, policy compliance, and user management.

 - Own Brisk's compliance calendar — track annual renewals across state registrations, foreign qualifications, registered agent filings, business licenses, and sales tax permits, ensuring nothing lapses.



Financial planning & analysis

 - Own monthly budget reviews with department budget owners, driving variance analysis and accountability.

 - Co-own the monthly finance review with our fractional CFO, surfacing trends, risks, and opportunities.

 - Prepare investor metrics and board-ready financial materials on a quarterly cadence.

 - Support annual and H2 planning cycles, including headcount modeling and opex forecasting.

 - Build and maintain financial models that support leadership decision-making and long-range planning.



Procurement & vendor management

 - Own vendor evaluation, renewals, and ongoing relationship management across the company's vendor portfolio.

 - Drive procurement process improvements that reduce spend and increase rigor.

 - Partner with Legal Ops on contracting, negotiation, and vendor compliance.



Cross-functional operations

 - Take on process improvement projects that increase operat

YOU WILL:

 - Lead Generation & Outreach: Identify and prospect new school and district accounts through targeted outreach, leveraging existing networks, and participating in educational events.

 - Sales Process Management: Guide prospective clients through the sales journey, from initial contact to closing, ensuring clear communication and a tailored approach.

 - Relationship Building: Develop strong relationships with education leaders, understanding their challenges and goals, and positioning Brisk’s offerings as valuable solutions.

 - Travel: Travel approximately 10-20% of the time to meet with prospective schools and districts, attend educational conferences in the territory, and represent Brisk at industry events.

 - Collaboration: Work closely with the Customer Success, Product, and Marketing teams to ensure a smooth onboarding process and a seamless transition from prospect to customer.

 - Market Insights: Gather insights from potential customers to share with our product and leadership teams, helping shape future product developments and strategies.

 - CRM Management: Accurately track and update prospect interactions and pipeline activity in our CRM system (e.g., Salesforce, HubSpot).

 - Achieve and Exceed Goals: Meet or exceed key performance indicators (KPIs), such as outreach targets, lead conversion rates, and revenue booking contributions.




YOU HAVE:

 - BA or BS in any field.

 - Sales Experience: 1–3 years of experience in inside sales, lead generation, or a related role, preferably in SaaS or EdTech.

 - Tools Experience: Prior familiarity, and ideally work experience, with Salesforce, Gong, and Outreach.

 - Communication Skills: Excellent written and verbal communication skills with the ability to connect with diverse stakeholders.

 - Customer-Focused: Passionate about understanding and solving customer challenges, with a genuine interest in K-12 education.

 - Self-Motivated: Ability to work independently and thrive in a fast-paced, evolving environment.

 - Team-Oriented: Comfortable collaborating with cross-functional teams to ensure the best outcomes for our clients and company.




BONUS:

 - Classroom teaching experience.

 - MEDDPICC certification.

Equal Employment Opportunity & Diversity

At Brisk, we believe that diverse perspectives make better teams and stronger products. We are an equal opportunity employer and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees.

notes: Rapid growth, AI platform for K-12 educators, mix of tech and non-tech roles
